title_en: George Sand’s life was a heady mix of sex and serious fiction
title_zh: 乔治·桑的生活在性与严肃文学之间，令人目眩
rubric_en: A new biography seeks to restore the French novelist’s place in the literary canon
rubric_zh: 一部新传记试图让这位法国小说家重返文学正典

paragraphs.en: HOW MUCH do you know about George Sand? If you are an Anglophone reader, the answer is probably “not a lot”. You might recall that she was a French writer and that “George Sand” was a pseudonym (her real name was Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin de Francueil). Perhaps you know that she wore men’s clothes and smoked cigars, or that she had a long affair with Frédéric Chopin, a celebrated composer. Only a few, however, will be able to name her works—and even fewer can claim to have actually read them. (Francophone readers, meanwhile, may have studied her books in school.)
paragraphs.zh: 你对乔治·桑了解多少？如果你是英语读者，答案大概是“不了解得不多”。你也许只知道她是法国作家，且“George Sand”是她的笔名（她本名叫阿曼丁·吕西尔·奥罗尔·杜潘·德·弗朗科耶）。或许你还记得她会穿男装、抽雪茄，或者她和著名作曲家弗雷德里克·肖邦有过一段长久的恋情。只有少数人能说出她的作品名字——能真正读过的人更少。（而法语读者可能在学校里读过她的书。）

paragraphs.en: With a new biography, Fiona Sampson, a poet, hopes to change that. She argues that Sand should be thought of as “one of the great novelists of the 19th century”. Sand was prolific—she had to be, given she supported herself and various lovers by her pen—and over the course of a 45-year career she produced more than 50 novels, scores of essays, memoirs and plays, as well as thousands of letters. Ms Sampson argues that the vast body of work was influential, too, probing the inner lives of her female characters and containing “some of the first ecological literature and pioneering fictions of rural life”.
paragraphs.zh: 随着一本新传记的出版，诗人菲奥娜·桑普森希望改变这种状况。她认为桑应被视为“19世纪最伟大的小说家之一”。桑创作异常丰产——她必须如此，因为她靠文字养活自己和多个情人——在四十五年的文学生涯中，她写出了五十多部小说，以及大量散文、回忆录和戏剧，还写下数以千计的书信。桑普森认为，这部庞大的作品体系同样具有影响力，深入探寻女性人物的内心世界，并包含“最早的生态文学写作”和“开拓性的乡村生活小说”。

paragraphs.en: Still, if Sand’s reputation has become “all personality, little art” it is mostly because she was an extraordinary person. Her life was eventful, scandalous even, from the get-go: her father, scion of a distinguished military family, married her mother, a former chorus girl, shortly before Sand was born. Her beloved father died in a riding accident when she was four. Many years later, when his grave was accidentally disturbed, Sand took the opportunity to clamber in and kiss his skull.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，桑的声誉之所以演变成“全部是传奇人物，艺术成分很少”，主要是因为她本身就是个与众不同的人。她的生活从一开始就多事且几乎带有丑闻色彩：她的父亲出身于显赫军人世家，却在桑出生前不久娶了她的母亲——一位前合唱团女孩。她最挚爱的父亲在她四岁时死于一次马术事故。许多年后，当父亲的坟墓被意外打扰时，桑抓住机会爬进去，亲吻了他的头骨。

paragraphs.en: After a spell in a convent as a teenager, Sand briefly considered taking religious vows. Instead she made vows of a different kind and, at 18, married a man who turned out to be boorish and violent. Before too long Sand left him—and her children—for Paris’s literary scene. She began collaborating with Jules Sandeau, a lover, and borrowing his name. But she soon struck out on her own. After an early hit with “Indiana”, publishers began competing for the rights to her work.
paragraphs.zh: 少女时期在修道院住了一阵子后，桑短暂考虑过是否要受戒。后来她转而做出了另一种“誓言”：18岁时她嫁给了一位后来被证明粗鲁而暴虐的男人。没过多久，桑就离开了他，也离开了自己的孩子，去到巴黎文学圈。她开始与情人朱尔斯·桑多合作，并借用了他的姓氏。可很快她就独立开展创作。凭借早期作品《印第安纳》获得成功后，出版商便开始竞相争夺她作品的出版权。

paragraphs.en: “Becoming George” is a romp to read. This is partly because Sand was a libertine, and an extremely successful one at that. (She is on holiday with one paramour when he falls ill; while he is recovering, she seduces the doctor who has come to care for him.) It is also because Ms Sampson is a skilful writer, who deftly evokes the cultural firmament in which Sand lived and worked. She has an enjoyable turn of phrase, too: one man is described as “a sexless creature, something of a bulgy-eyed haddock”. Typhoid, she muses, “must be quite an effective prophylactic”.
paragraphs.zh: 《成为乔治》读起来很轻快。这部分因为桑本人是个好色之徒，而且是非常成功的一类。（她在与一位情人度假时，他突然生病；在他养病期间，她勾引了来照顾他的医生。）也因为萨姆普森（Sampson）是一位功底扎实的作家，她熟练地重现了桑生存和写作所处的文化天幕。她文风也很有趣：她把某个男人形容为“一个没性欲的家伙，像一条眼球鼓鼓的鳕鱼”。她沉思道，伤寒“恐怕是种相当有效的避孕措施”。

paragraphs.en: The biography, however, falls short of its intended aim. At times Ms Sampson tells, rather than shows, the reader that Sand’s works matter; more in-depth literary analysis would have bolstered her argument. Sand’s personality remains more intriguing than her art. She emerges as an astonishingly modern hero—so much so that you wonder why there hasn’t been a Hollywood biopic about her in recent years.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，这本传记却没能完全达到其既定目标。有时，萨姆普森是在直接告诉读者桑的作品有价值，而不是通过文本本身来证明；如果有更深入的文学分析，她的论点会更有支撑。桑的个性仍然比她的艺术更耐人寻味。她呈现出一位令人震惊的现代英雄形象——以至于你会想，为什么近年来没有一部关于她的好莱坞传记电影？

paragraphs.en: Nevertheless, some readers may still be tempted to pick up a copy of “Indiana”, “Lélia” or “Mauprat” after they have finished “Becoming George”. For Ms Sampson lays out just how many admirers Sand had, including the Brontë sisters, Fyodor Dostoyevsky and Marcel Proust. Gustave Flaubert, who exchanged letters with Sand for many years, addressed his fellow writer as chère maître (dear master). After her death, he predicted that “Her name will live in unique glory as one of the great figures of France.” Any novelist with such a famous fanbase is surely worth a try. ■
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，一些读者在读完《成为乔治》后，仍可能会想翻开《印第安纳》《莉莉娅》或《莫普拉》一书。因为萨姆普森详细列出了桑的众多崇拜者，其中包括勃朗特姐妹、陀思妥耶夫斯基和马塞尔·普鲁斯特。加缪·福楼拜（Gustave Flaubert）与桑通信多年，曾称这位同道作家为“chère maître（亲爱的老师）”。在她去世后，他预言“她的名字将以独特的荣耀永存，作为法国伟大人物之一。”任何一位拥有如此知名读者群的小说家，都值得一试。■

paragraphs.zh: 想获取最新图书、电影、电视剧、专辑与争议内容，请订阅《Plot Twist》——我们的每周订阅者专属通讯。
